summary: Mixed show with standout Stranger>China Cat>Rider and strong drumming
blurb: Features a fiery Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence and solid drumming that many describe as a Harley‑factory roar. Friend Of The Devil shines in the first set while West L.A. Fadeaway offers a bright moment despite equipment hiccups.
review: On August 24, 1985 at Boreal Ridge Ski Resort the Dead delivered a typical mid‑'80s set that swings between brilliance and the frustrations of a hot, high‑altitude venue. The second set’s Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider chain blazes with Jerry’s tone cutting through the heat, and the dual‑drum segment earns rave comparisons to a Harley factory roar. First‑set highlights include a beautifully rendered Friend Of The Devil and an uplifting West L.A. Fadeaway that managed to shine despite monitor woes and a buzzing amp on Bob’s guitar. Brent’s keyboards provide color when they cut through the mix, while Phil wrestles with a wonky PA but holds down the low end. Overall the performance is uneven—some songs feel sluggish or off‑key—but the high points are memorable enough for Deadheads to give it a listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings describe the show as mixed and one is positive; overall sentiment leans neutral with a slight tilt toward positive due to repeated praise for the Stranger>China Cat>Rider sequence and the drumming, so a modest +0.2 adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with clear overall mix; includes the standout Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence despite some drum‑mix quirks, making it the cleanest and most complete source.

key_highlights: Stranger > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence electrifies the second set.
Mickey and Bill deliver an inventive, high‑energy drumming segment praised as a ‘Harley factory’ roar.
Friend Of The Devil is rendered beautifully in the first set.
West L.A. Fadeaway provides a bright spot despite monitor problems.

band_performance.Jerry: Mixed performance; moments of strong tone and energy are offset by slower, occasionally off‑key playing and a disengaged vibe at times.
band_performance.Phil: Struggles to lock in due to monitor issues; shows frustration but keeps the low end steady when possible.
band_performance.Bob: Plagued by amp buzz and occasional power loss, leading to stoppages that disrupt flow.
band_performance.Brent: Generally solid keyboard work; adds vocal harmonies and color, though often buried under venue noise.
band_performance.Mickey: Inventive, high‑octane drumming highlighted as a ‘Harley factory’ roar; one of the night’s strongest elements.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey for a tight, energetic drum segment that lifts the second set.
